NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-27-2019 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

CASE DISMISSED AS TO ENTIRE ACTION BY COURT AT ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING FEB-04-2020 AT 10:30 AM. THE COURT FINDS THAT A LESS SEVERE SANCTION WOULD NOT BE EFFECTIVE IN LIGHT OF THE HISTORY OF FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS AND RULES. NOTICE SENT BY COURT. *** PER ORDER VACATING DISMISSAL FILED ON MAR-13-2020***

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO VACATE DISMISSAL PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 473 (TRANSACTION ID # 100097565) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MARSILI, ROBERT , AN INDIVIDUAL HEARING SET FOR MAR-13-2020 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ROBERT MARSILI'S MOTION TO VACATE DISMISSAL PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 473: PLAINTIFF ROBERT MARSILI'S CCP 473(B) MOTION TO VACATE DISMISSAL IS GRANTED, WITH CONDITIONS. CALIFORNIA HAS A "STRONG PUBLIC POLICY FAVORING DISPOSITION ON THE MERITS." (BAHL V. BANK OF AMERICA (2001) 89 CAL.APP.4TH 389, 398.) PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY ANDREW DIMITRIOU DECLARES UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY: "I WAS SOLELY TO BLAME FOR THE FAILURE TO FILE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT AND RESULTING DISMISSAL." THUS, DIMITRIOU SHALL (1) FILE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ON OR BEFORE MARCH 18, 2020 AND (2) PAY- ON OR BEFORE APRIL 1, 2020 - $10,377 TO GOODMAN NEUMAN HAMILTON LLP AND $7,020 TO CHAPMAN LAW GROUP, APC FOR "REASONABLE COMPENSATORY LEGAL FEES AND COSTS." (SEE CCP 473(B).) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Dismissal Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 473
Off Calendar Mar-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Dimitriou, esq. (415-434-1144) for Plaintiff.
Tom Prountzos, esq. (415-705-0400) of Goodman Neuman Hamilton for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROBERT MARSILI's Motion To Vacate Dismissal Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 473

Plaintiff Robert Marsili's CCP 473(b) motion to vacate dismissal is granted, with conditions. California has a "strong public policy favoring disposition on the merits." (Bahl v. Bank of America (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 389, 398.) Plaintiff's attorney Andrew Dimitriou declares under penalty of perjury: "I was solely to blame for the failure to file the amended complaint and resulting dismissal." Thus, Dimitriou shall (1) file the amended complaint on or before March 18, 2020 and (2) pay- on or before April 1, 2020 - $10,377 to Goodman Neuman Hamilton LLP and $7,020 to Chapman Law Group, APC for "reasonable compensatory legal fees and costs." (See CCP 473(b).)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Rios Merida; Proceedings not reported. (610)
